description_long: This object is a large, modular concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. The barrier features a series of vertical rectangular segments with a slightly rough, matte gray surface, and the lower portion is stained or painted a sandy tan color, suggesting contact with soil or road debris. The top of the barrier is flat with notched sections, and there are visible metal lifting hooks embedded in the upper surface, indicating it is designed for repositioning with heavy machinery. The sides of the barrier display diagonal black stripes, which serve as high-contrast visual warnings for drivers and pedestrians. The overall construction appears solid and robust, with no visible openings or cavities, and the surface shows minor scuffs and weathering typical of outdoor use. This type of barrier is typically placed along roadsides, medians, or construction zones to prevent vehicle crossover and enhance safety.
description: A heavy, modular concrete jersey barrier with a gray matte finish, tan-stained base, and black diagonal warning stripes. It features metal lifting hooks and minor surface scuffs, designed for roadside or median placement.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the barrier, vertical segments, tan-stained base, and subtle surface scuffs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile and prominent black diagonal warning stripes along the length.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the segmented structure and tan-stained lower section.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and black warning stripes.
